attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a camera projection diagram with an image plane in the foreground-left, and a 3D target triangle in the background-right. The camera's coordinate origin is at the bottom-left of the image plane, with the viewing direction looking down and left towards this origin from a slightly elevated, right-of-center perspective.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows a very similar perspective looking down and left towards the coordinate origin, with the image plane on the left and the 3D target triangle on the right.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are highly consistent, though the rendered image plane appears slightly more vertically elongated/narrow due to perspective/orthographic projection settings.

shape_comparison.rendered_shape: wireframe rectangle of black tubes (no face fill)

shape_comparison.source_shape: flat orange triangle with black border
shape_comparison.rendered_shape: flat orange triangle with black tube border

shape_comparison.source_shape: dashed lines from coordinate origin through image points to 3D points
shape_comparison.rendered_shape: dashed lines from image points directly to 3D points

discrepancies: The projection rays (dashed lines) should originate from the coordinate origin (camera optical center) and pass through the image points p_i to the 3D points P_i. In the render, they are drawn directly between p_i and P_i, which misrepresents the camera projection geometry.
The image plane is missing a semi-transparent face fill, rendering only as a hollow wireframe box.
Severe label overlapping: 'Y_L' and 'p_3' are overlapping; 'Z_L', 'p_2', and 'p'_2' are all overlapping and highly cluttered at the bottom.
The coordinate axis arrow for Z_L is pointing in a slightly different direction, and its label is misplaced near p_2.
evaluation.notes: The overall visual structure is well captured, but critical camera projection geometry (rays originating from the optical center) is incorrect, and labels suffer from severe overlapping.
action_items: Reconstruct the dashed projection rays so they radiate from the camera coordinate origin, pass through the image plane points p_i, and terminate at the 3D target points P_i.
Add a semi-transparent face fill (e.g. MeshBasicMaterial with opacity 0.1) to the image plane rectangle to give it a physical surface appearance.
Adjust the HTML label offsets (particularly for Y_L, Z_L, p_2, p'_2, and p_3) to eliminate overlap and clutter.
Refine the camera/view parameters to match the aspect ratio of the original image plane shape more closely.

feedback.rationale: The geometry of the projection rays is conceptually incorrect, the image plane is missing its fill, and there is severe label overlapping, all of which are fixable action items for the generator.
action_items: Reconstruct the dashed projection rays so they radiate from the camera coordinate origin, pass through the image plane points p_i, and terminate at the 3D target points P_i.
Add a semi-transparent face fill (e.g. MeshBasicMaterial with opacity 0.1) to the image plane rectangle to give it a physical surface appearance.
Adjust the HTML label offsets (particularly for Y_L, Z_L, p_2, p'_2, and p_3) to eliminate overlap and clutter.
Refine the camera/view parameters to match the aspect ratio of the original image plane shape more closely.

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure displays a 3D camera calibration diagram from a front-right-above oblique viewpoint. The coordinate origin sits at the bottom-left, projecting rays through an intermediate image plane to a larger 3D triangle in the upper-right background.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot matches this layout precisely with an orthographic camera setup, showing the coordinate axes at the bottom-left, the image plane in the foreground, and the 3D triangle in the background.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are nearly identical, successfully replicating the 3D axonometric look of the textbook illustration.

shape_comparison.rendered_shape: flat triangle with thick tube edges
flat rectangle frame made of cylinders

discrepancies: The label 'p2' is displaced too far down, far away from the blue point it designates.
The small coordinate frame inside the image plane triangle is too thin and faint to be clearly visible.
The red reprojection error lines are slightly too thick compared to the thin red lines in the source.
evaluation.notes: Excellent visual faithfulness and geometric recreation, but lacks any custom interactive controls beyond basic OrbitControls and static annotations.
action_items: Add interactive sliders in the UI panel to adjust the positions of the 3D points P_i and watch the reprojection error update in real-time.
Adjust the position offset of label 'p2' to bring it closer to the blue point on the image plane.
Increase the thickness and visibility of the local coordinate axes inside the image plane triangle.
Thin out the red reprojection error lines and black dashed rays to look more delicate like the original source drawing.

feedback.rationale: The geometric and visual reproduction is excellent, but the figure lacks interactivity (scoring 1/5) and needs UI sliders to manipulate the 3D points alongside minor label and line-width adjustments.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a camera calibration setup with a tilted rectangular image plane on the left, a 3D target triangle on the right, and an origin coordinate system at the bottom-left camera center. The viewpoint is orthographic/isometric, looking from a slightly elevated and rightward position to clearly show the spatial separation between the image plane and the 3D points.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot matches this perspective closely using an orthographic camera view from a nearly identical azimuth and elevation, showing the image plane on the left, the coordinate axes at the bottom left, and the 3D triangle on the right with the same spatial layout.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are extremely well-aligned. The orthographic projection settings successfully replicate the diagrammatic look and feel of the original 2D textbook figure.

shape_comparison.rendered_shape: flat rectangle made of black tubes
flat triangular meshes with tube borders
3D spheres with outer black sphere shells

discrepancies: The label 'p2' at the bottom of the image plane is slightly crowded near the Z_L axis label.
The tiny green coordinate frame inside the image triangle is much smaller and less visible in the render compared to the source.
evaluation.notes: An exceptionally faithful recreation of the textbook figure with excellent interactive controls, highly accurate labels, and perfect conceptual clarity.
action_items: Adjust the offset of the 'p2' label to prevent minor crowding with the Z_L axis lines/labels.
Slightly increase the scale and thickness of the local green coordinate frame inside the image plane triangle to improve visibility.
Ensure the 'err' slider labels are slightly more polished in terms of CSS alignment if possible.

feedback.rationale: The rendered figure has achieved perfect scores across all categories with highly accurate geometry, labels, and interactivity, making further iteration unnecessary.
action_items: Adjust the offset of the 'p2' label to prevent minor crowding with the Z_L axis lines/labels.
Slightly increase the scale and thickness of the local green coordinate frame inside the image plane triangle to improve visibility.
Ensure the 'err' slider labels are slightly more polished in terms of CSS alignment if possible.
